ksa_text: Knowledge of AI security threats and adversarial ML techniques, including data poisoning, model extraction, evasion attacks, prompt injection, and the defensive measures and robustness testing approaches applicable to each.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.397) to justify inclusion for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Chief of Staff to CDAIO roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Chief of Staff to CDAIO roles increasingly include AI/ML Engineering competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Chief of Staff to CDAIO now include AI/ML Engineering competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. AI/ML Engineering knowledge KSA is relevant to the Chief of Staff to CDAIO based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
